title: Conversation about Mike's homeless friend's story of looking for work and why they deserve a chance

transcript.text: Hello, um, I'm doing a video talking about homeless people, um, because I'm going to have a little story to tell, um, I just wanted to say that, like, um, just with all the political stuff and attitude towards, you know, people who get food stamps and, um, people who beg, and, um, I don't think it's fair to say that they're, like, lazy and to just come to the conclusion.

So I think a lot of people, and just say we can get a job even, you know, just because you can see a lot of job offers, like on Craigslist and the newspaper, um, doesn't mean that it's easy to get work, you know, I, I tried to look for work for a while this summer and, um, you know, applied to a whole bunch of stuff and didn't get a job, you know, like, like internet stuff where you fill out an application, it takes like an hour to fill out and then they never, you know, contact you.

It's like they're, all these companies are collecting information from people. It seems kind of weird because it wastes people's time, but, but yeah, anyways, you know, you shouldn't just assume that people can get some good jobs who are out there with the signs trying to, you know, give money or, and the food stamps, which it cuts $40 billion off of this year.

Um, and, uh, yeah, so I just have a story of like my roommate, um, who I live with, uh, his, the roommate he had before that, um, is a, uh, moved in and then moved in with him and couldn't pay rent for a couple of years.

And, uh, in my roommate, it's nice enough to just let him stay there for free. Um, but the guy actually did try to get work, um, and, uh, you know, did everything to get the work, um, and, uh, just couldn't get it.
